strengths: Round 1: Strong opening with concrete value proposition - leading with the $2M annual savings from the payment system migration established credibility immediately
Round 2: Excellent use of multiple data points (market research, departing teammates, competing offer) to build a comprehensive case without being aggressive
Round 3: Smart pivot to replacement cost analysis when sensing resistance, showing business acumen by quantifying the cost of losing you
Round 4: Tactical concession from $195K to $192K while adding value through mentoring commitment - created a win-win structure
weaknesses: Round 1: Opened exactly at counterparty's reservation point ($195K) without leaving negotiation room - counterparty noted this was 'concerning'
Round 2: Failed to provide specific methodology details when directly asked, allowing counterparty to probe for weaknesses in your research
Round 3: Missed the counterparty's clear signal of readiness to negotiate when they acknowledged your replacement cost logic had 'merit'
missed_opportunities: Round 3: Counterparty was 'ready' to make concessions and found your replacement cost argument compelling, but you didn't capitalize by proposing specific next steps or package structures
Round 2: When counterparty questioned your individual contribution vs team effort on the $2M savings, you could have prepared specific examples of your unique role in the migration
suggested_adjustments: Start negotiations 10-15% above your target to create room for tactical concessions
Prepare detailed methodology and documentation for any market research claims before the negotiation begins
Recognize positive signals earlier - when counterparty validates your arguments, immediately propose concrete next steps
Have specific examples ready to defend individual contributions to team achievements
recommended_retry_focus: Learning to recognize and capitalize on counterparty concession signals - when they validate your arguments or show readiness, immediately propose concrete next steps rather than continuing to build your case
